entry.title: Combined summary - Regular signet reorgs
entry.summary: A programmer has developed a script to automate blockchain reorganizations (reorgs) on their signet miner, aiming for daily activations. The trigger mechanism for these reorgs is based on a cryptographically random event, involving the best block hash from the blockchain. Specifically, the script calculates a SHA-256 sum of a secret and the current best block hash, checking if the resulting hexadecimal value modulo 144 equals one. If this condition is met, the script initiates a reorganization process by invalidating the current block and subsequently forcing a new block to be mined. This method successfully triggered a two-block reorganization at block 307073, observable through tools like the signet network viewer provided by [fork.observer](https://fork.observer/?network=3). The puzzle for the community involves estimating the expected length of reorganizations considering that the mining intervals are random but constrained by a fixed time delay before mining can start. Additionally, there's an intellectual challenge to determine the hardcoded value of `$SECRET` used within the script. This scenario not only tests the robustness of blockchain technology under controlled conditions but also engages the community in solving complex cryptographic puzzles. Furthermore, the implementation captures data regarding coinbase tags from different miners involved in the network, which helps in identifying and analyzing contributions from various sources. This setup allows observers to track and understand the dynamics of miner interactions and behavior in a simulated environment, providing valuable insights into the decentralized decision-making processes inherent in blockchain technologies.
